evals.name: weekly-retrospective
evals.prompt: Rode /insights nas sessoes da ultima semana. Quero saber: quais erros se repetiram, quais regras foram violadas, e o que mudar pra melhorar.
evals.expected_output: Structured report with 4 phases: SCAN (error list from transcripts), AUDIT (rules cross-ref), DIAGNOSE (categorized findings), PRESCRIBE (concrete proposals with diffs). Should cite specific sessions and never auto-apply changes.

assertions.text: Reads session JSONL transcripts (not just CHANGELOG)
assertions.check: transcript shows grep or read of .jsonl files

assertions.text: Cross-references findings against .claude/rules/ files
assertions.check: transcript shows reading at least 3 rule files

assertions.text: Categorizes findings using taxonomy (RULE_VIOLATION, RULE_GAP, etc.)
assertions.check: report uses at least 2 distinct category tags from the taxonomy

assertions.text: Each proposal cites specific session evidence
assertions.check: proposals reference session IDs or dates, not vague 'sometimes'

assertions.text: Proposals are concrete and diff-ready (not vague suggestions)
assertions.check: at least 1 proposal includes actual text to add/change in a specific file

assertions.text: Does NOT auto-apply changes to rules/skills/hooks
assertions.check: no Edit/Write calls to .claude/rules/ or .claude/skills/ (except insights own references/)

assertions.text: Does NOT modify memory files (dream's domain)
assertions.check: no Edit/Write calls to memory/ directory

assertions.text: Saves report to insights/references/latest-report.md
assertions.check: Write call to references/latest-report.md

evals.name: focused-error-patterns
evals.prompt: Quais erros se repetem nas minhas sessoes recentes? Foca nos patterns que aparecem 2+ vezes.
evals.expected_output: Quick focused report listing recurring error patterns with frequency counts, session citations, and top 5 proposed fixes. Should be faster than full retrospective.

assertions.text: Identifies at least 2 distinct error patterns
assertions.check: report lists multiple categorized patterns

assertions.text: Shows frequency (how many sessions each pattern appeared in)
assertions.check: each pattern has a count or 'N sessions' indicator

assertions.text: Cites specific sessions for each pattern
assertions.check: session dates or IDs appear alongside patterns

assertions.text: Proposes fixes for top patterns
assertions.check: at least 2 patterns have proposed fixes

assertions.text: Shorter output than full retrospective (focused mode)
assertions.check: report is concise, no full rule audit section

assertions.text: Scans JSONL transcripts, not just CHANGELOG
assertions.check: transcript shows grep/read of .jsonl files

assertions.text: Does NOT auto-apply changes
assertions.check: no Edit/Write to rules/skills/hooks

evals.name: rule-health-check
evals.prompt: Audita as rules em .claude/rules/ — quais estao sendo seguidas, quais nao, quais estao desatualizadas ou tem gaps.
evals.expected_output: Compliance matrix showing each rule with status (followed/violated/stale/gap). Identifies rules that reference things that no longer exist, and gaps where errors happened with no covering rule.

assertions.text: Reads ALL rule files in .claude/rules/
assertions.check: transcript shows reading all .md files in .claude/rules/

assertions.text: Produces a compliance matrix with status per rule
assertions.check: report has a table or list with each rule + status tag

assertions.text: Identifies at least 1 stale or outdated rule element
assertions.check: report flags something as RULE_STALE or outdated

assertions.text: Identifies at least 1 gap (error pattern with no covering rule)
assertions.check: report flags something as RULE_GAP

assertions.text: Checks rules against session evidence (not just reading the rule text)
assertions.check: transcript shows searching sessions for rule-related patterns

assertions.text: Does NOT auto-modify any rule files
assertions.check: no Edit/Write to .claude/rules/

assertions.text: Proposes concrete updates for stale/gap rules
assertions.check: at least 1 proposal with specific text to add/change
